slides_data.prompt: Dramatic dark background with shattered glass effect revealing red warning symbols, bold typography space, violet and coral accent particles, cinematic tension, 4:5
slides_data.headline: 6 BEGINNER MISTAKES

slides_data.prompt: Person staring at empty plate with extreme deficit, sad expression, dark moody blue lighting, dramatic portrait, calorie counter showing dangerously low number, 4:5
slides_data.headline: 1. CUTTING CALORIES TOO LOW
slides_data.body:
Under 1200? Your metabolism slows down.
You lose muscle, not just fat.
Fix: Moderate deficit (300-500 cal).
Slow and steady wins every time.

slides_data.prompt: Protein powder and supplements scattered chaotically while real food sits ignored in background, dark contrast lighting, red X overlay, 4:5
slides_data.headline: 2. IGNORING PROTEIN
slides_data.body:
Most beginners eat half what they need.
Protein preserves muscle during cuts.
Protein builds muscle during bulks.
Fix: Track it. Aim for 1.6-2.2g per kg.

slides_data.prompt: Person on treadmill for hours looking exhausted, gym clock showing long duration, dark dramatic lighting, sweat and fatigue, 4:5
slides_data.headline: 3. TOO MUCH CARDIO
slides_data.body:
Cardio doesn't build the body you want.
Excessive cardio = muscle loss + hunger.
Fix: Prioritize lifting. Add cardio strategically.
3-4 sessions per week. Not 7.

slides_data.prompt: Calendar showing inconsistent tracking with random gaps, dark background, missed days highlighted in red, scattered pattern, 4:5
slides_data.headline: 4. TRACKING ONLY ON GOOD DAYS
slides_data.body:
Monday to Thursday? Perfect.
Weekend? Total mystery.
The weekend is where most diets fail.
Fix: Track everything. Even the bad days.

slides_data.prompt: Person weighing themselves daily with anxious expression, scale showing different numbers each day, dark background with red fluctuation graph, 4:5
slides_data.headline: 5. OBSESSING OVER DAILY WEIGHT
slides_data.body:
Weight fluctuates 1-2 kg daily. Water, sodium, stress.
Daily weigh-ins = daily anxiety.
Fix: Track weekly averages.
Trends matter. Single days don't.

slides_data.prompt: Person copying someone else's meal plan from social media, generic one-size-fits-all diet sheet, dark background, mismatched body types, 4:5
slides_data.headline: 6. COPYING SOMEONE ELSE'S PLAN
slides_data.body:
Their body isn't yours.
Their metabolism isn't yours.
Their lifestyle isn't yours.
Fix: Get a plan built for YOU. AI-personalized.

slides_data.prompt: Phone showing Cals2Gains with personalized dashboard, all 6 mistakes crossed out with green checkmarks, dark premium UI, violet and coral celebratory glow, 4:5
slides_data.headline: FIX ALL 6 TODAY
slides_data.body: Cals2Gains personalizes everything with AI. Free 7 days.
